cooperates.cooperate_desc: Johnson and Argus can create a High-Impact "Global Dive" Combo. Johnson's Ultimate can transport Argus Directly to the enemy Backliner such as Mages and Marksmans, Bypassing the frontliners entirely. Once Johnson crashes into an enemy, his 9/10 Control Effects stun them long enough for Argus to activate his ultimate and secure multiple kills before the enemy can react.

dominants.dominant_desc:
1. Early Game (Laning Phase)
Phase Name: Early Game / Laning

Tips: Focus on clearing minions and reaching Level 4 as fast as possible. If you're a Jungler, prioritize your buffs. If you're in a lane, poke the enemy but stay near your turret to avoid ganks

2. Mid Game (Roaming & Objectives)
Phase Name: Mid Game / Ganking

Tips: Start rotating to other lanes to help your teammates. Secure the Turtle whenever it spawns. This is the time to finish your core items and start forcing small skirmishes.

3. Late Game (Team Fights & Lord)
Phase Name: Late Game / Final Push

Tips: Stick with your team—getting caught alone now usually means a loss. Focus on the enemy's main damage dealer (Marksman/Mage) in team fights. Group up to take the Lord and push for the win.

emblems.emblem_desc: I chose this set to maximize raw damage and critical strikes. Fatal increases my Crit Chance and Damage, while Weapon Master scales my physical attack from items by 5%. War Cry provides a significant damage boost after consecutive hits, making this the perfect setup for shredding tanks and high-HP targets in the late game.
You can delete enemies in late game

hero.hero_overview:
## Skill Overview
### Passive: Warmonger
Argus possesses a Malice Energy bar that charges up as he deals or takes damage. When the bar is full, his next basic attack strikes twice, dealing extra damage and healing him.

Pro Tip: This is why Attack Speed items are non-negotiable for him.

### Skill 1: Demonic Grip
Argus launches a demonic hand in a target direction. If it hits an enemy or a wall, he pulls himself toward them and can cast the skill again to dash a second time.

Usage: This is your primary gap closer and escape tool.

### Skill 2: Meteoric Sword
Argus stabs forward with his sword. Enemies hit take damage and are "cursed" for several seconds. Cursed enemies leave a dark trail behind them; when Argus walks on this trail, he gains a significant Movement Speed boost.

Usage: Use this to stick to enemies who try to flicker or run away.

### Ultimate: Eternal Evil
This is his signature move. Argus transforms into a fallen angel, removing all debuffs and becoming immune to death for 4 seconds (at max level).

The Kicker: 100% of the damage dealt to enemies during this state is converted into HP for Argus once the duration ends.
hero.hero_strength: Argus is a Late-Game Hyper-Carry whose main strength is being "literally immortal" for a window of time
hero.hero_weakness:
Despite being "immortal," Argus has some major flaws that smart players exploit:

Crowd Control (CC) is His Nightmare: If he gets stunned, suppressed (Kaja/Franco), or frozen (Aurora) right when he pops his Ultimate, he can't hit anyone. Since he heals by dealing damage during his Ult, stunning him = no healing

Kiting & Mobility: Once his Ultimate is active, enemies usually just run away. If he can't reach you with his Skill 1 (Demonic Grip), he wastes his 4-5 seconds of immortality swinging at the air.

Weak Early Game: Before he gets 2 or 3 core items (like Great Dragon Spear or Corrosion Scythe), his damage is actually pretty low. He is very easy to bully in the laning phase

Item Counters: * Dominance Ice: Reduces his insane attack speed and healing.
Wind of Nature (WON): Marksmen can pop this to become immune to his physical damage for 2 seconds, wasting half of his Ultimate duration

data.title: Argus guide

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Cc is the death of an Argus main, the moment your locked down, that's 1 second wasted of Ult, 1 second worth of damage gone, and 1 enemy healthbar that hasn't been depleted yet. Diggie synergizes way too well with way too many heroes, Argus doesn't have anything special to offer, but Diggie is such a clutch up for those cc heavy enemy lineups.

counters.counter_desc: Yu Zhong is simply too tanky, and too sustain heavy. Argus simply stands no chance against a Yu Zhong player who has more than a few braincells.
Terizla is too tanky and too sustain heavy for Argus to deal with. It's better to just try and win lane, and get to late game.

Too agile to even catch, and even then if you catch a Cici her sustain and tanky nature is an absolute hard counter to you. Just give up try to be on par with a Cici and start picking off their marksmen and mages to make sure you don't lose as hard.

Gloo has great sustain and crazy tankyness, it's honestly better off for you to get targeted by his ult and run away rather than your own teammates who are most likely more important to the team than you.

Literally the Princess of dashes, Argus is absolutely shut down by any kind of CC and sustain, he relies heavily on his enemy or target to be squishy and exhausted of dashes. Absolute hard counter to Argus.
Just pull the trigger lil bro. He's stronger, he's tankier, and he's way better than you in teamfights. If you chose Argus after a Lapu pick, you honestly deserve the ass whooping coming to you.

dominants.dominant_desc: Contrary to popular belief, late game Argus isn't the only thing that's useful, mid game Argus however is very different from late game Argus. Mid game Argus doesn't focus on teamfights, he focuses on being a constant presence the enemy has to watch out for. You play him sneakily, minimizing your time spent on the enemy's minimap, picking off the marksman or mage, the core if possible but they'll be playing extra cautious to score objectives, and if you can or the enemy is in the middle of their rotation, start pushing like all hell, this guarantees the enemy becomes more wary of the minimal, trying to locate your presence and hopefully diverting their attention to you rather than your team.

His gameplay shifts drastically, you now the one that decides when a teamfight can be won easily or elbow grease is gonna have to be put on. Your goal is to dive into enemies far away from base, even if there are 5 of them, and cause as much destruction as possible before dying. Get them to use mobility skills, ults, battle spells, everything, do these the few moments before an objective starts. Exhaust the enemies and if you can get a kill or two, even better. Constantly pressure lanes by rotation from top to bottom and vice versa by split pushing, divide the enemies attention and ascertain that the moment they reach your team, they are heavily unprepared.

emblems.emblem_desc: This emblem setup focuses on bursting down enemies with crit builds. I highly recommend this emblem setup as it helps burst down, or at the very least weaken major players on the enemy team, such as the Jungler, Marksman, or Mage

This emblem setup is built towards the attack speed and chase Argus playstyle, while it doesn't differ much from dive in and die, it is harder to burst down with this and usually leaves the enemy you caught with S1 on a sliver of health.

equips.equip_desc:
NOTE: ONCE YOU HAVE THE FULL BUILD REPLACE WINDTALKER WITH CORROSION SCYTHE AND SWIFT BOOTS WITH A HIGH DAMAGE ITEM LIKE BLADE OF DESPAIR OR HUNTER STRIKE

This build primarily focuses on bursting down key figures in the enemy lineup, grappling onto them with S1 and bursting them down with 2 enhanced basic attacks using skill 2.

What else can be said about it? It's old reliable, tank melter and a presence that makes enemies save one mobility skill, although I doubt you'd be able to perform kills before the enemies could react.

hero.hero_overview: Argus, considered one of the WORST heroes in MLBB and rightfully so, Immortality means nothing if the enemy simply doesn't let you do anything with it. This guide will hopefully be able to help you understand the foundation of decision making with Argus.
hero.hero_strength: Argus' main strength comes from his ult's immortality and stun purify. Argus is strong when it comes to 1v1s, risky plays (as if playing him wasn't risk enough), and his ability to choose fights.
hero.hero_weakness: Argus is incomparably weak to any other hero, sometimes even detrimental to the team with insane sustain existences like Yu Zhong who can heal off of you for free. Argus also has immense trouble when faced off against high mobility heroes, immortality doesn't help you run faster after all.

spell.spell_desc: Inspire is honestly the best battle spell for Argus. Argus is essentially an all or nothing hero, the moment you dive in or commit to an enemy, you will essentially die if you chicken out halfway through. That's why inspire is a very good spell, it lets you kill faster and slightly delay using his ult (more on that later). As opposed to Flicker which is basically a chase or escape to Argus, likely the former, Inspire fully embraces his dive in, kill the enemy, die playstyle.

hero.hero_overview: Argus is a durable fighter in Mobile Legends who excels at diving into the backline and sustaining through fights. He deals significant physical damage and can heal himself through lifesteal, making him a strong presence in prolonged team fights and split-pushing scenarios. His skills are straightforward, allowing him to poke, engage, and finish off enemies efficiently. His ultimate ability, Eternal Evil, lets him become temporarily immortal, turning the tide of battles by allowing him to continue fighting even after taking lethal damage.
hero.hero_strength: Argus strengths lie in his high sustain and strong team fight presence. His lifesteal combined with his skill damage allows him to stay in fights longer than most fighters. He is especially effective against squishy heroes such as marksmen and mages, and his ultimate gives him the ability to dive into the enemy backline safely. Additionally, Argus has a relatively simple learning curve, making him accessible for new players while still being impactful in higher-level play.
hero.hero_weakness: However, Argus has some notable weaknesses. He is vulnerable in the early game before he levels up and gains lifesteal, making him susceptible to harassment and ganks. He is also highly dependent on his ultimate to survive team fights, and without it, he can be easily controlled or kited by enemies with crowd control or mobility. Playing Argus effectively often requires good timing and coordination with teammates, as engaging too early or without support can lead to quick deaths.

spell.spell_desc: Inspire temporarily increases your attack speed and basic attack damage for a few seconds. This boost allows Argus to deal more damage quickly, especially during his ultimate, where he's immortal for a short time. Using Inspire right as you engage in fights or dive into the enemy backline maximizes his DPS (damage per second) and makes him more threatening to squishy targets.

spell.spell_desc:
In Mobile Legends  Inspire is a battle spell that enhances your Basic Attacks for a short period Here a breakdown of what Inspire does and how it works

What Inspire Does

Increases Attack Speed significantly for several seconds
Basic Attacks ignore part of the enemy  Physical Defense making your hits deal more damage

Grants lifesteal in some cases depending on the hero items or passive


Current Inspire Effect 
as of 2024

Cooldown 60 seconds

Effect

Boosts your attack speed mmscales with hero level

Increases Basic Attack damage by ignoring part of the target
defense

Lasts for 8 seconds

Gives 8 enhanced Basic Attacks during the spell duration 


When to Use Inspire

During duels or team fights to burst down enemies

If he ult first you win
If you ult first you dead

But if she uses ult run or act as a tank so your ranged teamate can kill her.

Can clap in seconds.
If she uses crit build stay alert as she can use ult to approach you and ******** in seconds
Dont let him ult you against a wall as that stun is same as being suppresed.
Stay away from fighting her in bush until you get anti heal.
His ring skill is annoying as not only it slows movement speed but also attack speed.

1st skill should be used to engage , chase or retreating.
2nd skill is used to slow and get passive.
Ult should be used at 50% hp or so to get the most time out of his ult buffs.(activating ult too early may cause enemy to run from you and make you a free kill when ult ends)

The dragon spear can be bought anytime except for being 1st and 2nd equipment for the needed speed boost.

This build is for when enemy has alot of tanks.Haas claw can be changed to berserker or golden staff.(note that if change to golden staff should be bought right after demon sword). And take note that it has very crappy crit rate .

hero.hero_overview: Argus is a late game hero that can deal high dmg . His play style is that you should not be agressive until you get some items and farm. Pls remember to help turtle too :) .
hero.hero_strength: Late game can clap most heroes . Can pretty much kill most marksman in seconds late game.and can also tank hits cause of immortality.
hero.hero_weakness: Argus is weak againt stun heroes and also weak in early game.Heroes that counter him in xp lane are sustain and usually are yu zhong , terizla and ruby. Although his stun has a purify , he still cant escape suppresion from franco , barats and kaja.(for franco just ult the moment the hook hits you  and run away with 1st skill when stun ends).

spell.spell_desc: Inspire is best for argus since his ult has a purify and he becomes unkillable.Inspire also gives argus sustain he lacks early game and matches well with his passive.

equips.equip_desc: This build focuses on attack speed and critical hits, which are essential for Argus to maximize his damage output. Since Argus benefits from continuous attacks to charge his Demonic Blade and recover health, this build enhances his sustainability and burst potential.

hero.hero_overview: Argus is a powerful fighter in Mobile Legends, known for his high durability in battles thanks to his ultimate skill, which makes him temporarily invincible. He deals high physical damage and gains increased attack speed over time, making him a serious threat in both solo and team fights. His playstyle is aggressive, focusing on engaging enemies and landing continuous strikes.
hero.hero_strength:
His ultimate skill, "Eternal Evil," makes him invincible for a short duration, allowing him to deal massive damage without fear of dying.

High physical damage and increasing attack speed over time.

Can regenerate health when attacking enemies, improving his sustainability in fights.

Excellent for one-on-one duels (1v1) due to his high burst damage.

Has good chase potential with his ability to gain extra movement speed.
hero.hero_weakness:
Vulnerable to crowd control effects (Stun, Slow, Silence), which can disable him before using his ultimate.

Highly dependent on the proper timing of his ultimate; if misused, he can be easily eliminated.

Difficult to manage in team fights without proper support from teammates.

Relies on offensive items to be effective, making him weak in the early game.


This makes Argus a strong duelist but requires good strategy and positioning to maximize his potential.

spell.spell_desc:
Spell Description:
Inspire has been chosen to enhance Argus's offensive power. This spell significantly increases his attack speed for a short duration, allowing him to land more strikes quickly and restore HP when attacking enemies. It perfectly suits Argus's playstyle, which relies on continuous attacks and maximizing his "Eternal Evil" ultimate ability to deal massive damage without worrying about death.
